deep_dive.title: Meta-Modal Agent: Sequential Evidence Routing for Missing-Modality Candidate Reranking
deep_dive.abstract: Missing modalities cause severe failures in multimodal recommender systems. User histories, item text, and visual evidence are frequently absent during cold-start scenarios, exactly when recommendation quality matters most. Existing approaches recover absent signals through imputation, feature propagation, or generative reconstruction, but these strategies can inject unsupported evidence when the surviving signals are weak. We introduce the Meta-Modal Agent (MMA), a large language model based candidate-pool reranker that treats missingness as a sequential evidence-routing problem. MMA is trained with balanced missingness-task reinforcement learning over masked-modality episodes and is evaluated in two variants: MMA-Auto, which uses only automated text, image, and graph tools, and MMA-Interactive, which additionally permits clarification questions grounded in surviving modalities as an upper-bound diagnostic. MMA operates after a first-stage retriever has produced a candidate pool; it scores those candidates rather than retrieving items from the full catalog. Final reranking fuses MMA scores with first-stage retrieval scores selected on validation data. Our evaluation is organized around four evidence checks required for a robust missing-modality claim: oracle-free one-observed-modality availability (OOMA) robustness, per-modality OOMA breakdowns, fixed-pool full-catalog reranking, and a deterministic-router mechanism control. MMA-Auto improves target-positive OOMA NDCG@10 by 4.0% and fixed-pool full-catalog reranking NDCG@10 by 12.7% over the strongest non-interactive baseline. RuleRouter-Fuse, which uses the same tools and fusion rule without learned policy updates, underperforms MMA-Auto, supporting learned routing beyond deterministic tool fusion. MMA-Interactive adds a 4.1% upper-bound gain when clarification is available.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based reranker trained with reinforcement learning for missing modality scenarios
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于大语言模型的重排序器，使用强化学习训练处理缺失模态
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于大语言模型的重排序器，使用强化学习训练处理缺失模态
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于大语言模型的重排序器，使用强化学习训练处理缺失模态
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Meta-Modal Agent treats missing modalities as a sequential evidence-routing problem, using a large language model reranker trained with reinforcement learning on masked-modality episodes, outperforming baselines.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态推荐中模态缺失会导致严重性能下降，现有方法可能引入虚假证据。本文提出Meta-Modal Agent，将缺失视为序列证据路由问题，使用大语言模型作为候选池重排序器，并采用基于缺失任务均衡的强化学习训练。实验表明，MMA在冷启动场景下显著优于基线方法，为处理模态缺失提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多模态推荐中模态缺失会导致严重性能下降，现有方法可能引入虚假证据。本文提出Meta-Modal Agent，将缺失视为序列证据路由问题，使用大语言模型作为候选池重排序器，并采用基于缺失任务均衡的强化学习训练。实验表明，MMA在冷启动场景下显著优于基线方法，为处理模态缺失提供了新范式。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 关于reranker训练方法的研究, especially work that connects or combines: reranker training methods; cross encoder training for reranking; pointwise reranker training; listwise reranker training; ranking model training techniques; loss function used in reranker training; how to train a reranker for information retrieval; training methodology of cross encoder reranker; loss functions used in reranker training.

deep_dive.title: Compute Allocation in Evolutionary Search: From Depth-Breadth to Multi-Armed Bandits
deep_dive.abstract: LLM-guided evolutionary search (Evolve systems) has reached state-of-the-art results on mathematical and combinatorial tasks, yet most existing systems report only the best of many runs and leave the run-to-run distribution undocumented. We ask how a fixed budget of LLM calls should be allocated, and how reliably a single run reaches the reported numbers. Sweeping the depth-breadth grid over five models and three tasks, we identify two empirical regularities: a fitness-compute envelope along which capability ordering largely collapses on effective FLOPs, and a bilinear depth-breadth fit with task-specific interaction; both are gated by model-task capability. Motivated by these regularities, we propose BaSE (Bandit-based Self-Evolving), a multi-armed bandit that allocates LLM calls across parallel trajectories. Without changing the model, prompt, or evaluator, BaSE improves mean fitness by 12.3% over the strongest island-protocol baseline across 8 (model, task) cells, with the largest gains on high-variance settings: a reliability gain from allocation alone.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-guided evolutionary search for automatic discovery on math tasks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 大模型引导的进化搜索用于数学任务自动发现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 大模型引导的进化搜索用于数学任务自动发现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 大模型引导的进化搜索用于数学任务自动发现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Studies compute allocation in LLM-guided evolutionary search (Evolve systems) and proposes BaSE, a bandit-based self-evolving algorithm, advancing automatic discovery with LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有大模型引导进化搜索（Evolve系统）在数学和组合任务上取得最优结果，但缺乏对运行分布和计算预算分配的深入分析。本文揭示了深度-广度网格的两个经验规律，并提出基于多臂老虎机的自适应进化搜索算法BaSE，有效分配LLM调用预算。实验表明BaSE在固定预算下能更可靠地达到高性能，为自动发现算法提供了高效的计算分配策略。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有大模型引导进化搜索（Evolve系统）在数学和组合任务上取得最优结果，但缺乏对运行分布和计算预算分配的深入分析。本文揭示了深度-广度网格的两个经验规律，并提出基于多臂老虎机的自适应进化搜索算法BaSE，有效分配LLM调用预算。实验表明BaSE在固定预算下能更可靠地达到高性能，为自动发现算法提供了高效的计算分配策略。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: automatic discovery algorithms combining LLM and evolutionary computation

deep_dive.title: ProjectionBench: Evaluating Scientific Hypothesis Generation in LLMs Under Progressive Information Disclosure
deep_dive.abstract: Scientific discovery is an inherently creative and uncertain process, requiring reasoning beyond the recall of known knowledge. While many benchmarks have been proposed to evaluate large language model (LLM) performance on deep research tasks via multi-hop retrieval, their innovative reasoning abilities essential for true scientific discovery remain largely untested. We introduce a benchmark framework for evaluating model performance in scientific discovery and reasoning, building up from a raw problem to the classical null hypothesis test. In our framework, models initially receive only the topic and research question from a recent paper, with technical details progressively revealed. At each stage of information disclosure, the model is tasked with generating hypotheses that address the research question, which is compared with the conclusions from the original paper and evaluated via automated semantic similarity of constituent atomic claims. This progressive evaluation of semantic divergence from ground-truth conclusions enables assessment of a model's innovativeness (under minimal information) to grounded reasoning capabilities (under full experimental details), both critical for using LLMs for scientific discovery purposes. Our framework provides a foundation for systematically evaluating scientific reasoning and discovery capabilities in LLMs, crucial for advancing the development of next-generation AI scientist/co-scientist systems. Specifically, here we evaluate GPT-5, GPT-5.4, Gemini 2.5 pro, and Gemini 3.1 pro preview across 45 papers spanning bioactive materials, mechanical materials, and nanomaterials. We find that GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 pro outperform their previous generation counterparts as expected, and GPT-5.4 in particular maintains 0.7 F1 score alignment with ground truth conclusions even under minimal context.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evaluates LLMs' capability for scientific hypothesis generation, directly addressing discovery tasks with LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估大语言模型在科学假设生成方面的能力，直接涉及利用大语言模型进行发现任务
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估大语言模型在科学假设生成方面的能力，直接涉及利用大语言模型进行发现任务
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估大语言模型在科学假设生成方面的能力，直接涉及利用大语言模型进行发现任务
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a benchmark framework that progressively reveals information to evaluate LLM's hypothesis generation and reasoning for scientific discovery, testing innovative reasoning beyond recall.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出ProjectionBench基准，在渐进信息披露下评估大语言模型的科学发现与推理能力。框架从原始问题开始逐步揭示细节，测试模型提出原创假设和设计检验的能力。实验表明现有模型在真正创新推理上仍有差距。该基准为评估LLM在科学发现中的推理能力提供了新方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出ProjectionBench基准，在渐进信息披露下评估大语言模型的科学发现与推理能力。框架从原始问题开始逐步揭示细节，测试模型提出原创假设和设计检验的能力。实验表明现有模型在真正创新推理上仍有差距。该基准为评估LLM在科学发现中的推理能力提供了新方法。

deep_dive.title: Joint Optimization of Relevance and Engagement in Multi-Task Ranking for E-Commerce with Efficient LLM Supervision
deep_dive.abstract: Optimizing industrial search ranking models solely for user engagement signals often introduces systematic biases, prioritizing popular or price-anchored items that may not satisfy semantic intent. We present a production-scale multi-task ranking system that integrates semantic relevance as a primary optimization objective, enabling explicit and controllable relevance-engagement trade-offs. Our architecture employs an ordinal relevance head that predicts cumulative probabilities over relevance thresholds, preserving the inherent ordering of labels. These outputs are integrated with engagement heads through a unified value model scoring function, enabling systematic balancing of semantic quality and short-term behavioral signals. To provide high-quality supervision for this multi-task framework, we utilize fine-tuned lightweight Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate three-level ordinal relevance labels: irrelevant, moderately relevant, and highly relevant. We address challenges regarding label distribution sensitivity and ensure high alignment with human annotations to enable efficient labeling for over 100 million query-item pairs. Evaluation across offline metrics, including NDCG@10, and online A/B experiments demonstrates that our approach significantly improves semantic alignment while preserving core engagement objectives.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-task ranking system with LLM supervision for e-commerce search
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM监督的多任务排序系统用于电商搜索
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用LLM监督的多任务排序系统用于电商搜索
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM监督的多任务排序系统用于电商搜索
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a production-scale multi-task ranking system integrating semantic relevance and engagement, with an ordinal relevance head and LLM supervision, enabling controllable trade-offs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对工业搜索排序模型仅优化用户参与信号导致的偏差问题，本文提出多任务排序系统，将语义相关性作为主要优化目标，采用序数相关头预测累积概率，并与参与头通过统一价值模型整合。利用大模型进行高效监督，实现语义质量与短期指标的系统性平衡。在电商场景验证了其有效性。

deep_dive.title: LRanker: LLM Ranker for Massive Candidates
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently shown strong potential for ranking by capturing semantic relevance and adapting across diverse domains, yet existing methods remain constrained by limited context length and high computational costs, restricting their applicability to real-world scenarios where candidate pools often scale to millions. To address this challenge, we propose LRanker, a framework tailored for large-candidate ranking. LRanker incorporates a candidate aggregation encoder that leverages K-means clustering to explicitly model global candidate information, and a graph-based test-time scaling mechanism that partitions candidates into subsets, generates multiple query embeddings, and integrates them through an ensemble procedure. By aggregating diverse embeddings instead of relying on a single representation, this mechanism enhances robustness and expressiveness, leading to more accurate ranking over massive candidate pools. We evaluate LRanker on seven tasks across three scenarios in RBench with different candidate scales. Experimental results show that LRanker achieves over 30% gains in the RBench-Small scenario, improves by 3-9% in MRR in the RBench-Large scenario, and sustains scalability with 20-30% improvements in the RBench-Ultra scenario with more than 6.8M candidates. Ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of its key components. Together, these findings demonstrate the robustness, scalability, and effectiveness of LRanker for massive-candidate ranking.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based reranker for large-scale candidate ranking with aggregation encoder
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于大语言模型的大规模候选排序重排序器，使用聚合编码器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于大语言模型的大规模候选排序重排序器，使用聚合编码器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于大语言模型的大规模候选排序重排序器，使用聚合编码器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LRanker, an LLM-based reranker with candidate aggregation encoder and graph-based test-time scaling for large-candidate ranking.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型在排序任务中展现潜力，但受限于上下文长度和计算成本，难以应用于数百万候选集的场景。本文提出LRanker框架，利用K-means聚类实现候选聚合编码，并通过图式测试时缩放机制将候选集分区，生成多个查询嵌入集成排序。实验表明LRanker在大规模排序中有效，为训练高效重排序器提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型在排序任务中展现潜力，但受限于上下文长度和计算成本，难以应用于数百万候选集的场景。本文提出LRanker框架，利用K-means聚类实现候选聚合编码，并通过图式测试时缩放机制将候选集分区，生成多个查询嵌入集成排序。实验表明LRanker在大规模排序中有效，为训练高效重排序器提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: GRASP: Plan-Guided Graph Retrieval with Adaptive Fusion and Reranking on Semi-Structured Knowledge Bases
deep_dive.abstract: Semi-structured knowledge bases (SKBs) embed textual documents in a typed graph of entities and relations, and underpin applications such as product search, academic paper search, and precision-medicine inquiries. Existing hybrid retrieval systems on SKBs either use the graph only for query expansion, mix textual and structural branches under a global weighting, or rely on fine-tuned graph-traversal generators. We present GRASP, a three-stage SKB retrieval framework unifying plan-based graph retrieval, plan-conditioned fusion with a dense retriever, and a fine-tuned reranker over the fused candidates. GRASP substantially advances the state of the art on every metric across the three STaRK benchmarks, lifting average Hit@1 from 62.0 to 73.9. Ablation and sensitivity studies further confirm the effectiveness and robustness of GRASP.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对半结构化知识库的微调重排器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对半结构化知识库的微调重排器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对半结构化知识库的微调重排器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GRASP is a three-stage retrieval framework for semi-structured knowledge bases that includes a fine-tuned reranker, achieving state-of-the-art on STaRK benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对半结构化知识库检索中混合检索系统的局限性，本文提出GRASP框架，包含基于计划的图检索、条件融合和微调重排器。在STaRK基准上取得最优结果，表明重排器训练对提升检索质量至关重要。该方法为知识库重排器的训练提供了有效范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对半结构化知识库检索中混合检索系统的局限性，本文提出GRASP框架，包含基于计划的图检索、条件融合和微调重排器。在STaRK基准上取得最优结果，表明重排器训练对提升检索质量至关重要。该方法为知识库重排器的训练提供了有效范式。
